title: Vanquo
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of Vanquo|the similarly named planet of [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]]}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Vanquo.jpg]] |name=Vanquo |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} *[[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]]Vanquo's placement on the [[Mandalorian Road]] and other maps in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', in relation to [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]], places it south of the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] region as defined by ''The Essential Atlas''. |sector=[[Meerian sector/Legends|Meerian sector]] |system=[[Vanquo system/Legends|Vanquo system]] |coordinates=O-6 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons=At least [[Unidentified moon (Vanquo)|1]][[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]] |routes=[[Mandalorian Road]] |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=*[[Mountain/Legends|Mountains]] *[[Forest/Legends|Forests]] |water= |interest=*[[Unidentified aid station|Aid station]] *[[Unidentified mining outpost|Mining outpost]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' }} {{Quote|I'll tell ya, if the Mandalorians ever move on Vanquo, they'll find nothing here but people they've already seen!|A Vanquo miner, regarding the refugee situation on the planet|}} '''Vanquo''' was a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Meerian sector/Legends|Meerian sector]] that became the site of one of the [[Battle of Vanquo|early battles]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. A [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] planet, Vanquo was a resource world for the nearby [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and a haven for [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] from the early conquests of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Army/Legends|armies]]. When Taris was threatened by the Mandalorians, Vanquo became part of a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] security cordon known as the [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]]. In [[3964 BBY]], Vanquo was attacked by the Mandalorians in force, commanded by [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] and his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Cassus Fett]]. After a heated [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]] with [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] forces led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]], the Mandalorians conquered Vanquo, giving them a stronghold that they did not relinquish during the war. ==Description== {{Quote|—just look around. Trees. Mountains. ''Sky!''|Jarael|}} Located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Meerian sector/Legends|Meerian sector]], Vanquo was a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] and a resource world for the nearby [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Situated in a [[Vanquo system/Legends|system]] of the same name,{{CSWECite|III|294|Vanquo}} Vanquo was located along the [[Mandalorian Road]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] region, and was within traveling distance of other worlds, such as [[Suurja]] and [[Jebble]]. A rocky, [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] planet covered in forests and [[Tree/Legends|trees]], Vanquo was a rugged world with a harsh [[Winter/Legends|winter]]. On clear [[Standard day/Legends|nights]], Vanquo had a brilliant [[Star/Legends|starry]] sky, with at least one [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Unidentified moon (Vanquo)|moon]] visible from the planet's surface. ==History== {{Quote|It's working, ''Mand'alor''. The Republic fleet's left flank has split to cover ''Vanquo''—clearing the way for our main thrust!''"
"''Then the path to ''Taris'' is clear, at last.|Cassus Fett and Mandalore the Ultimate discuss their attack on Vanquo|[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]}} In [[3976 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]], led by their [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], began to carve out an empire by conquering unaligned worlds in the Outer Rim, far from the sight of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. After amassing a sizable amount of territory, [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] launched out toward the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], contesting resource worlds near Taris, including Vanquo. One of the worlds caught in the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] advance, Vanquo became part of a [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] security cordon known as the [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]], along which heavy, stalemated fighting occurred in the early days of what became known as the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. [[File:Mandalorians_invade_Vanquo.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Mandalorians invade Vanquo.]] In [[3964 BBY]], Vanquo, by now dotted with mining camps, had become a destination for many [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] uprooted by the Mandalorian crusades. Although the Mandalorians loomed, business continued as usual for the mining crews across Vanquo. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Vanquo was visited by wanted fugitive [[Zayne Carrick]], accused of the [[Padawan Massacre|massacre]] of his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|classmates]] on Taris, and his crew as they tried to lay low from [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] pursuit. Using Carrick's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Mind trick/Legends|talents]] and his [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] friend [[Jarael]]'s convincing portrayal of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Q'Anilia]], the group was able to convince the staff of a [[Unidentified mining outpost|mining outpost]] that the Mandalorians were attacking. The crew abandoned the camp, leaving it and all its resources to the hungry fugitives. However, the Mandalorians, led by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Rohlan Dyre]], began their actual [[Battle of Vanquo|attack]] soon afterward with a large [[Trooper/Legends|paratrooper]] landing, as Carrick and his friends were still gathering supplies—although Jarael was mistaken for a real Jedi and was captured, the others were able to escape. The battle was personally coordinated by Mandalore the Ultimate and his [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Cassus Fett]], and was mainly a diversionary tactic, meant to draw Republic forces and open the way for Mandalore's main thrust toward Taris. On the other side, the Republic forces were led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]] aboard the [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|battleship]] [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']]. As the mining camps on the night side of the planet were seized and an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] screen was being set up so the Mandalorians could attack the major settlements on the day side, a major [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]] raged over Vanquo. The Vanquo offensive turned out to be a rousing success, as the left flank of the main Republic fleet was drawn to Vanquo, clearing the way to Taris, and the Mandalorians ended up seizing the planet. Over the course of the conflict, Vanquo proved to be a stronghold that the Republic was never able to break. In the aftermath of the Mandalorian Wars, Republic heroes [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]] turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and led a new [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] [[Jedi Civil War|against]] the Republic. Although Vanquo never fell within [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith borders]], in [[3956 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] led an offensive toward Taris, resulting in a Sith victory and the complete [[Destruction of Taris|devastation]] of the planet, a push that started at Vanquo. In the later years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Vanquo's region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] fell within the [[Zsinj's Empire|empire]] of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] beginning in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invaders [[Battle of the Vanquo system|attacked]] Vanquo after their successful [[Attack on the Bandomeer system|conquering]] of [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]]. The Yuuzhan Vong won the battle and continued their offensive on toward the Core. By [[137 ABY]], Vanquo fell within the territory of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|Caught another blasted refugee sneaking around the camp again.|A miner on Vanquo|}} By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Mandalorian Wars, Vanquo had become inundated with a large number of refugees fleeing the looming Mandalorian menace, many of whom had come from nearby battlegrounds, such as [[Jebble]] and [[Suurja]]. With Vanquo already possessed of a sizable population of miners at camps spread across the world, many of these hungry, desperate refugees attempted to plunder from the miners' stores. There was also a civilian population mainly centered at several major settlements on the other side of the planet from the mining camps. ==Locations== {{Quote|You want help, there's an aid station on the other side.''"
"''Other side? Of the ''mountains?"
"''Of the ''planet,'' stench! Start walking if you want to beat winter!|Miner [[Zem (miner)|Zem]] and a disguised Zayne Carrick|}} At the time of the Mandalorian Wars, there were a number of mining camps spread out across one side of the planet, nestled within Vanquo's natural mountainous terrain. There was a communications system set up between some of the various mining settlements, with an official Republic channel to provide necessary information to the outposts. On the other side of the planet were several major settlements, along with an [[Unidentified aid station|aid station]] frequented by refugees from the Mandalorian attacks. ==Behind the scenes== The planet Vanquo made its first appearance in [[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2006]]. The planet was also seen in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|next installment]] of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] series— in both its appearances, Vanquo was illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]]. Vanquo was mentioned in a number of other ''Knights of the Old Republic'' issues[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]] and stories based on the comic series.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/interference20090929.html|text="Interference"|int=Interference|newhost=Suvudu}} More information on Vanquo and the Vanquo [[Star system/Legends|system]] were given in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and its [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|online companion]], along with its entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances==
